summary: FPV drones were used by Russian forces to intercept over 2,000 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators from the "Rubicon" Center accounting for more than 80 enemy UAV models, over 75% of which were fixed-wing. On April 11, 2026, "Rubicon" destroyed 110 targets, including fixed-wing UAVs and personnel, while coordinating with the Russian Aerospace Forces to strike Ukrainian UAV launch points. Ukraine's 24th Brigade tested a ceasefire by sending disguised POWs, who were immediately struck by Russian FPV drones, killing three of their own soldiers. Ukrainian forces survived a Russian FPV attack in the Kostiantynivka direction, using drone detectors to anticipate the strike. A new portable drone, the Small Tactical Unmanned Drone (STUD), was reported with a 300 km/h speed and 64 km range, featuring a foldable design similar to the Switchblade concept.
